enumDescriptions: The value of the numerical field will be used to dynamically update the boost amount. In this case, the attribute_value (the x value) of the control point will be the actual value of the numerical field for which the boost_amount is specified.

For the freshness use case the attribute value will be the duration between the current time and the date in the datetime field specified. The value must be formatted as an XSD `dayTimeDuration` value (a restricted subset of an ISO 8601 duration value). The pattern for this is: `nDnM]`. E.g. `5D`, `3DT12H30M`, `T24H`.
attributeType.description: Optional. The attribute type to be used to determine the boost amount. The attribute value can be derived from the field value of the specified field_name. In the case of numerical it is straightforward i.e. attribute_value = numerical_field_value. In the case of freshness however, attribute_value = (time.now() - datetime_field_value).

If an app with the same `app_id` already exists, its content will be updated based on the imported app. - Resources (App, Agents, Tools, Examples, Guardrails, Toolsets) in the imported app that have the same display name as existing resources will overwrite the existing ones. - Imported resources with new display names will be created. - Existing resources that do not have a matching display name in the imported app will remain untouched.

Overwrite existing data with imported data. If an app with the same `app_id` already exists, its content will be overwritten with the imported app. - Existing resources (Agents, Tools, Examples, Guardrails, Toolsets) in the app will be deleted. - Imported resources will be created as new resources.
conflictResolutionStrategy.description: Optional. The strategy to use when resolving conflicts during import.

ImportAppRequestImportOptions.description: Configuration options for the app import process. These options control how the import behaves, particularly when conflicts arise with existing app data.

supportedLanguageCodes.description: Optional. List of languages codes supported by the app, in addition to the `default_language_code`.

defaultLanguageCode.description: Optional. The default language code of the app.

enableMultilingualSupport.description: Optional. Enables multilingual support. If true, agents in the app will use pre-built instructions to improve handling of multilingual input.

fallbackAction.description: Optional. The action to perform when an agent receives input in an unsupported language. This can be a predefined action or a custom tool call. Valid values are: - A tool's full resource name, which triggers a specific tool execution. - A predefined system action, such as "escalate" or "exit", which triggers an EndSession signal with corresponding metadata to terminate the conversation.

nextPageToken.description: A token that can be sent as ListEvaluationsRequest.page_token to retrieve the next page. Absence of this field indicates there are no subsequent pages.

GuardrailLlmPromptSecurity.description: Guardrail that blocks the conversation if the input is considered unsafe based on the LLM classification.

defaultSettings.description: Optional. Use the system's predefined default security settings. To select this mode, include an empty 'default_settings' message in the request. The 'default_prompt_template' field within will be populated by the server in the response.

failOpen.description: Optional. Determines the behavior when the guardrail encounters an LLM error. - If true: the guardrail is bypassed. - If false (default): the guardrail triggers/blocks. Note: If a custom policy is provided, this field is ignored in favor of the policy's 'fail_open' configuration.

customPolicy.description: Optional. Use a user-defined LlmPolicy to configure the security guardrail.

nextPageToken.description: A token that can be sent as ListScheduledEvaluationRunsRequest.page_token to retrieve the next page. Absence of this field indicates there are no subsequent pages.

scheduledEvaluationRuns.description: The list of scheduled evaluation runs.

pythonCode.description: Required. The python code to execute.

PythonCodeCondition.description: Python code block to evaluate the condition.

ImportEvaluationsRequestImportOptions.description: Configuration options for the evaluation import process. These options control how the import behaves, particularly when conflicts arise with existing evaluations data.

conflictResolutionStrategy.description: Optional. The strategy to use when resolving conflicts during import.

Overwrite the existing evaluation with the new one.

EvaluationScenarioExpectation.description: The expectation to evaluate the conversation produced by the simulation.

toolExpectation.description: Optional. The tool call and response pair to be evaluated.

agentResponse.description: Optional. The agent response to be evaluated.

args.description: Optional. The input parameters and values for the tool in JSON object format.

displayName.description: Output only. Display name of the tool.

toolsetTool.description: Optional. The toolset tool to execute.

tool.description: Optional. The name of the tool to execute. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/apps/{app}/tools/{tool}`

id.description: Optional. The unique identifier of the tool call. If populated, the client should return the execution result with the matching ID in ToolResponse.

ToolCall.description: Request for the client or the agent to execute the specified tool.

description.description: Optional. Description of the tool's purpose.

rootAgent.description: Optional. The resource name of the root agent that is the entry point of the tool. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/agents/{agent}`

name.description: Required. The name of the agent tool.

AgentTool.description: Represents a tool that allows the agent to call another agent.

AgentAgentToolset.description: A toolset with a selection of its tools.

toolset.description: Required. The resource name of the toolset. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/apps/{app}/toolsets/{toolset}`

toolIds.description: Optional. The tools IDs to filter the toolset.

chunks.description: Optional. Content of the message as a series of chunks.

role.description: Optional. The role within the conversation, e.g., user, agent.

eventTime.description: Optional. Timestamp when the message was sent or received. Should not be used if the message is part of an example.

EndSession.description: Indicates the session has terminated, due to either successful completion (e.g. user says "Good bye!" ) or an agent escalation. The agent will not process any further inputs after session is terminated and the client should half-close and disconnect after receiving all remaining responses from the agent.

metadata.description: Optional. Provides additional information about the end session signal, such as the reason for ending the session.

nextPageToken.description: A token that can be sent as ListConversationsRequest.page_token to retrieve the next page. Absence of this field indicates there are no subsequent pages.

LatencyReportToolLatency.description: Latency metrics for a single tool.

toolsetTool.description: Output only. The toolset tool identifier.

toolDisplayName.description: Output only. The display name of the tool.

latencyMetrics.description: Output only. The latency metrics for the tool.

nextPageToken.description: A token that can be sent as ListAppVersionsRequest.page_token to retrieve the next page. Absence of this field indicates there are no subsequent pages.

audioGcsUri.description: The Google Cloud Storage URI where the uploaded audio file is stored. Format: `gs:///`

transcript.description: The transcript of the audio, generated by Cloud Speech-to-Text.

toolsetTool.description: The toolset tool that the schema is for.

inputSchema.description: The schema of the tool input parameters.

outputSchema.description: The schema of the tool output parameters.

tool.description: The name of the tool that the schema is for. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/apps/{app}/tools/{tool}`

EndUserAuthConfig.description: End-user authentication configuration used for Connection calls. The field values must be the names of context variables in the format `$context.variables.`.

oauth2JwtBearerConfig.description: JWT Profile Oauth 2.0 Authorization Grant authentication.

EvaluationResultSpanLatency.description: The latency of a span execution.

callback.description: Output only. The name of the user callback span.

displayName.description: Output only. The display name of the span. Applicable to tool and guardrail spans.

resource.description: Output only. The resource name of the guardrail or tool spans.

endTime.description: Output only. The end time of span.

type.description: Output only. The type of span.
enumDescriptions: Default value. This value is unused.

executionLatency.description: Output only. The latency of span.

model.description: Output only. The name of the LLM span.

toolset.description: Output only. The toolset tool identifier.

startTime.description: Output only. The start time of span.

EvaluationDataset.description: An evaluation dataset represents a set of evaluations that are grouped together basaed on shared tags.

etag.description: Output only. Etag used to ensure the object hasn't changed during a read-modify-write operation. If the etag is empty, the update will overwrite any concurrent changes.

evaluations.description: Optional. Evaluations that are included in this dataset.

updateTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the evaluation dataset was last updated.

name.description: Identifier. The unique identifier of this evaluation dataset. Format: `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/apps/{app}/evaluationDatasets/{evaluationDataset}`

aggregatedMetrics.description: Output only. The aggregated metrics for this evaluation dataset across all runs.

createdBy.description: Output only. The user who created the evaluation dataset.

lastUpdatedBy.description: Output only. The user who last updated the evaluation dataset.

displayName.description: Required. User-defined display name of the evaluation dataset. Unique within an App.

createTime.description: Output only. Timestamp when the evaluation dataset was created.

averageLatency.description: Output only. The average latency of the turns.

totalCount.description: Output only. Total number of evaluation results in this run.

completedCount.description: Output only. Number of evaluation results that finished successfully. (EvaluationResult.execution_state is COMPLETED).

passedCount.description: Output only. Number of completed evaluation results with an outcome of PASS. (EvaluationResult.execution_state is COMPLETED and EvaluationResult.evaluation_status is PASS).

errorCount.description: Output only. Number of evaluation results that failed to execute. (EvaluationResult.execution_state is ERROR).

failedCount.description: Output only. Number of completed evaluation results with an outcome of FAIL. (EvaluationResult.execution_state is COMPLETED and EvaluationResult.evaluation_status is FAIL).

EvaluationRunProgress.description: The progress of the evaluation run.

McpToolset.description: A toolset that contains a list of tools that are offered by the MCP server.

serverAddress.description: Required. The address of the MCP server, for example, "https://example.com/mcp/". If the server is built with the MCP SDK, the url should be suffixed with "/mcp/". Only Streamable HTTP transport based servers are supported. See https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/transports#streamable-http for more details.

serviceDirectoryConfig.description: Optional. Service Directory configuration for VPC-SC, used to resolve service names within a perimeter.

apiAuthentication.description: Optional. Authentication information required to access tools and execute a tool against the MCP server. For bearer token authentication, the token applies only to tool execution, not to listing tools. This requires that tools can be listed without authentication.

customHeaders.description: Optional. The custom headers to send in the request to the MCP server. The values must be in the format `$context.variables.` and can be set in the session variables. See https://docs.cloud.google.com/customer-engagement-ai/conversational-agents/ps/tool/open-api#openapi-injection for more details.

tlsConfig.description: Optional. The TLS configuration. Includes the custom server certificates that the client should trust.

enableCloudLogging.description: Optional. Whether to enable Cloud Logging for the sessions.

CloudLoggingSettings.description: Settings to describe the Cloud Logging behaviors for the app.

startTime.description: Required. Timestamp when the eval should start.

daysOfWeek.description: Optional. The days of the week to run the eval. Applicable only for Weekly and Biweekly frequencies. 1 is Monday, 2 is Tuesday, ..., 7 is Sunday.

frequency.description: Required. The frequency with which to run the eval

callCount.description: Output only. The number of times the resource was called.

GoogleSearchTool.description: Represents a tool to perform Google web searches for grounding. See https://cloud.google.com/customer-engagement-ai/conversational-agents/ps/tool#google-search.

contextUrls.description: Optional. Content will be fetched directly from these URLs for context and grounding. Example: "https://example.com/path.html". A maximum of 20 URLs are allowed.

name.description: Required. The name of the tool.

description.description: Optional. Description of the tool's purpose.

preferredDomains.description: Optional. Specifies domains to restrict search results to. Example: "example.com", "another.site". A maximum of 20 domains can be specified.

promptConfig.description: Optional. Prompt instructions passed to planner on how the search results should be processed for text and voice.

excludeDomains.description: Optional. List of domains to be excluded from the search results. Example: "example.com". A maximum of 2000 domains can be excluded.

WebSearchQuery.description: Represents a single web search query and its associated search uri.

uri.description: The URI to the Google Search results page for the query.
